markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
Will New Zealand win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
Will Switzerland win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
Will England win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
Will Team AM win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France share the narrowest edge in trader consensus for the 2026 FIFA World Cup title, driven by their status as European champions and consistent tournament performers with exceptional squad depth. Spain benefits from Luis de la Fuente’s young core featuring Lamine Yamal and strong recent Nations League form, while France leverages Didier Deschamps’ experienced roster and attacking options despite some pre-tournament injury concerns. England follows closely on the back of flawless qualification and back-to-back European final appearances, creating a tightly bunched top tier. South American sides like Argentina and Brazil trail amid questions over aging stars and form, with the expanded 48-team field and imminent kickoff on June 11 amplifying uncertainty around fitness and matchups that keeps implied probabilities clustered among the leading contenders.

markets.question: Will Rafael López Aliaga win the 2026 Peruvian presidential election?
Will Carlos Álvarez win the 2026 Peruvian presidential election?
Will César Acuña win the 2026 Peruvian presidential election?
Will Vladimir Cerrón win the 2026 Peruvian presidential election?
Will Roberto Chiabra win the 2026 Peruvian presidential election?

eventMetadata.context_description: Keiko Fujimori holds a commanding 93.5% implied probability in this runoff market due to her first-round plurality of 17%, consistent strength among urban and coastal voters, and emphasis on security measures against rising crime during a period of political instability. The June 7 contest against Roberto Sánchez features a narrow official count that remains too close to call, with rural and Andean ballots continuing to favor the leftist candidate while international votes trend toward Fujimori. Late shifts in remaining tallies, disputes over rural turnout, or procedural delays by electoral authorities could still alter the outcome before final certification.

title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?

markets.question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by April 22, 2026?
US x Iran permanent peace deal by April 30, 2026?
US x Iran permanent peace deal by May 31, 2026?
US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 30, 2026?
US x Iran permanent peace deal by April 24, 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Negotiations between the United States and Iran, mediated primarily by Pakistan, continue amid a conditional ceasefire extended into mid-2026 following months of conflict that began in February. Recent statements from President Trump in late May indicated that a memorandum of understanding on ending hostilities is largely negotiated, with core elements including phased reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a temporary pause on Iranian uranium enrichment, potential sanctions relief, and discussions on highly enriched uranium disposal. Progress has been incremental, with Iranian proposals addressing war termination first and nuclear issues later, though final approvals remain pending and prior deadlines have passed without resolution. These diplomatic exchanges, alongside regional involvement from countries including Saudi Arabia and Qatar, shape trader assessments of whether a permanent agreement can be reached by specific 2026 dates.
